Description: A surrogate primary key used to uniquely identify the system information record.
The specific version number or build identifier of the database schema.
The date and time when the current database version was released or deployed.
The date and time when the version record was last updated.
Unique identifier for each log entry.
The date and time when the database change was executed.
The database user account that performed the action.
The type of DDL event that triggered the log entry (e.g., CREATE_TABLE, ALTER_INDEX).
The database schema to which the affected object belongs.
The name of the database object (table, view, procedure, etc.) that was created or modified.
The exact Transact-SQL command that was executed.
The complete event data in XML format, providing detailed context about the environment and the change.
Primary key for the error log entry
The date and time when the error occurred
The database or application user who encountered the error
The specific SQL Server error number
The severity level of the error (typically 0-25)
The state code of the error, used to identify the specific location in the code that raised the error
The name of the stored procedure or trigger where the error occurred
The line number within the procedure or trigger where the error was raised
The full text description of the error message
